questions.question: The Fundamental Duties of citizens are enshrined in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: In Part IVA.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the West Bengal Panchayat Raj System?
options: Panchayat Samitis are Intermediate bodies between the Zilla Parishad and Gram Panchayats.
Panchayat Samitis are Intermediate bodies between the Gram Panchayats and Block Development Officers.
Zilla Parishad is the highest unit of Panchayat Raj in West Bengal.
The West Bengal Panchayat Raj system does not have any Intermediate bodies between the Zilla Parishad and Gram Panchayats.
questions.explanation: Panchayat Samitis are Intermediate bodies.

questions.question: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendments of 1992 introduced which of the following?
options: Reservation of seats for SC and ST in Municipalities and Panchayats.
Reservation of seats for women in Municipalities and Panchayats.
Direct elections for Chairmen of Zilla Parishads and Municipalities.
Reservation of seats for SC and ST in Parliament and State Legislatures.
questions.explanation: Reservation of seats for women.

questions.question: Who is the Chief Minister of West Bengal, as per the West Bengal Ministers (Salaries, Allowances and Miscellaneous Laws) Amendment Act, 2018?
options: Chief Minister to be the Chairman of the West Bengal Public Service Commission.
Chief Minister to be the Chancellor of the universities in West Bengal.
Chief Minister to be the Chairman of the West Bengal Infrastructure Development Finance Corporation.
Chief Minister to be the President of the West Bengal Legislative Assembly.
questions.explanation: Chairman of the West Bengal Public Service Commission.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental right?
questions.explanation: Right to Freedom.

questions.question: Which river is the main tributary of the Ganges in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Hooghly River is the main distributary of the Ganges in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following hill stations is located in the Darjeeling Hills of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Darjeeling is a famous hill station in the Darjeeling Hills of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following islands is located off the coast of Kolkata, West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sagar Islands are a group of islands located off the coast of Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges separates the districts of Jalpaiguri and Alipurduar in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Naga Hills separate the districts of Jalpaiguri and Alipurduar in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Singalila Range in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Tista River originates from the Singalila Range in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which Bengaluru-based startup acquired a majority stake in Bengal-based e-learning platform, Toppr in 2025?
questions.explanation: Byju's acquired a majority stake in Toppr.

questions.question: The West Bengal government has launched a project to provide piped natural gas in how many districts of the state?
questions.explanation: The West Bengal government launched a project to provide piped natural gas in 7 districts.

questions.question: Who is the chairman of the West Bengal Board of Secondary Education (WBBSE) that was reconstituted in 2025?
questions.explanation: Bratya Basu is the chairman of the WBBSE.

questions.question: The Centre has approved the West Bengal government's proposal to develop which tourist circuit under the Swadesh Darshan scheme?
questions.explanation: The Centre approved the Sundarbans Circuit under the Swadesh Darshan scheme.

questions.question: Which Indian city is hosting the Global Bio-India 2025 conference, which was attended by the West Bengal chief minister in 2025?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is hosting the Global Bio-India 2025 conference.

questions.question: A shopkeeper sold an article at Rs. 450, which was bought for Rs. 350. What is the profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is calculated by (profit/selling price) * 100.

questions.question: In a certain year, the cost price of 10 kg wheat was Rs. 50. If the cost price per kg is increased by 20%, then what will be the cost price of 15 kg wheat?
questions.explanation: The new cost price per kg is 1.2 times the original cost price per kg.

questions.question: A toy car is sold at a loss of 10% and the selling price is Rs. 420. What is the cost price?
questions.explanation: The cost price is calculated by (selling price) / (1 - loss percentage) = (selling price) / (1 - 0.10).

questions.question: A person buys 5 kg rice at Rs. 50 per kg and sells 3 kg rice at Rs. 60 per kg. What is the profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is calculated by (profit/selling price) * 100.

questions.question: A company increases the price of its product by 25% in a certain year. If the original price was Rs. 1000, what will be the new price?
questions.explanation: The new price is 1.25 times the original price.

questions.question: A train travelling from Howrah to Sealdah covers a distance of 20 km in 1 hour, whereas the distance between the two stations is 40 km. What is the remaining time to cover the remaining distance?
questions.explanation: The remaining distance is 20 km which can be covered in 1 hour.

questions.question: A person walks from Howrah Station to Esplanade at a speed of 3 km/h, and returns at a speed of 2.5 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Average speed is the total distance divided by the total time.

questions.question: A bus starts from Kolkata and travels to Digha at an average speed of 60 km/h. If the distance between Kolkata and Digha is 200 km, how much time will it take for the bus to reach Digha?
questions.explanation: Time is distance divided by speed.

questions.question: A person travels from Kolkata to Kalyani at a speed of 30 km/h. If the time taken for the journey is 2 hours, how much distance has been covered?
questions.explanation: Distance is speed multiplied by time.

questions.question: A car travels from Howrah to Binnaguri at an average speed of 80 km/h. If the time taken for the journey is 2.5 hours, what is the distance covered?
questions.explanation: Distance is speed multiplied by time.

questions.question: Which of the following will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given coding: gurukulam ? aab ? bengal?
questions.explanation: The given coding is done by using the first letter of the word.

questions.question: How many letters are there in the first word of the given phrase: 'The main motto of West Bengal government is to provide justice'.
questions.explanation: The first word is 'The'.

questions.question: Which of the following will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given coding: bengal ? d ? kolkata?
questions.explanation: The given coding is done by using the first letter of the word.

questions.question: Find out the number of letters in the given word: 'Sankrail'.
questions.explanation: The number of letters in 'Sankrail' is 7.

questions.question: Which of the following will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given coding: river ? g ? howrah?
questions.explanation: The given coding is done by using the first letter of the word.

questions.question: Kolkata-based Amar and his wife are parents of a son. Amar's mother is the grandmother of his son. What is the relationship between Amar's mother and Amar's son?
questions.explanation: Amar's mother is the grandmother of her son.

questions.question: A person, Sagnik, has a father and a maternal uncle. His maternal uncle is the brother of his mother. What is the relationship between Sagnik and his maternal uncle?
questions.explanation: Sagnik's maternal uncle is the brother of his mother.

questions.question: A person, Aniket, has a brother and a maternal aunt. His maternal aunt is the sister of his mother. What is the relationship between Aniket and his maternal aunt?
questions.explanation: Aniket's maternal aunt is the sister of his mother.

questions.question: A person, Sayan, has two sisters. His brother-in-law is the husband of his sister. What is the relationship between Sayan and his brother-in-law?
questions.explanation: Sayan's brother-in-law is the husband of his sister.

questions.question: A person, Abhishek, has a sister and a brother-in-law. His brother-in-law is the husband of his sister. What is the relationship between Abhishek and his brother-in-law?
questions.explanation: Abhishek's brother-in-law is the husband of his sister.

questions.question: A person is facing towards the south direction. He turns 180 degrees to his left. In which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He turns 180 degrees to his left, so he is facing the opposite direction.

questions.question: A man starts walking from the Howrah Bridge towards the Ganges River. After 200 metres, he turns left towards the city. Which direction is he heading now?
questions.explanation: He turned left after walking towards the Ganges River.

questions.question: A person is standing in front of a mirror in his home in Burdwan. He is facing towards the north direction. Which direction will he see in the mirror?
questions.explanation: He will see his own reflection, which is opposite to his actual direction.

questions.question: A person is walking from the Alipore Zoo towards the Indian Museum. After 500 metres, he turns right towards the city. Which direction is he heading now?
questions.explanation: He turned right after walking towards the Indian Museum.

questions.question: A man is standing in front of the Dakshineswar Kali Temple in Kolkata. He turns 90 degrees to his right. In which direction is he facing now?
questions.explanation: He turns 90 degrees to his right, so he is facing the adjacent direction.
